edges.description: ECM-gene variants weaken the aortic wall matrix.
edges.source: Germline pathogenic variant in an HTAD gene

edges.description: TGF-beta pathway gene variants perturb growth-factor signaling.
edges.source: Germline pathogenic variant in an HTAD gene

edges.description: Contractile-apparatus gene variants impair smooth-muscle force generation and mechanosensing.
edges.source: Germline pathogenic variant in an HTAD gene
edges.target: Vascular smooth muscle contractile apparatus disruption

edges.description: Matrix failure weakens the aortic wall and promotes dilation and dissection.
Dysregulated TGF-beta signaling contributes to maladaptive aortic wall remodeling.
Impaired contractility and stress sensing increase susceptibility to aneurysm and dissection.
edges.source: Vascular smooth muscle contractile apparatus disruption

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in genes such as FBN1, LOX, and COL3A1 compromise aortic extracellular matrix organization, elastic-fiber integrity, or collagen structure, weakening the thoracic aortic media.

evidence.reference_title: HTAD patient pathway: Strategy for diagnostic work-up of patients and families with (suspected) heritable thoracic aortic diseases (HTAD). A statement from the HTAD working group of VASCERN.
evidence.snippet: Associated genes identified to date are classified into those encoding components of the (a) extracellular matrix (b) TGFβ pathway and (c) smooth muscle contractile mechanism.

nodes.description: Inherited pathogenic variants in high-penetrance HTAD genes initiate familial thoracic aortic aneurysm and dissection risk. Downstream mechanisms cluster into extracellular matrix, TGF-beta signaling, and vascular smooth muscle contractile pathways.
nodes.id: Germline pathogenic variant in an HTAD gene

evidence.reference_title: HTAD patient pathway: Strategy for diagnostic work-up of patients and families with (suspected) heritable thoracic aortic diseases (HTAD). A statement from the HTAD working group of VASCERN.
evidence.snippet: They may result from genetic defects.

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in TGFBR1, TGFBR2, SMAD3, or TGFB2 perturb transforming growth factor beta signaling, contributing to thoracic aortic wall remodeling and aneurysm susceptibility.

biological_processes: transforming growth factor beta receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: HTAD patient pathway: Strategy for diagnostic work-up of patients and families with (suspected) heritable thoracic aortic diseases (HTAD). A statement from the HTAD working group of VASCERN.
evidence.snippet: Associated genes identified to date are classified into those encoding components of the (a) extracellular matrix (b) TGFβ pathway and (c) smooth muscle contractile mechanism.

nodes.description: Progressive thoracic aortic enlargement increases wall stress and can lead to acute aortic dissection or rupture. Because relatives can have silent dilation, imaging surveillance is clinically important.

evidence.reference_title: Evaluating the Feasibility of Screening Relatives of Patients Affected by Nonsyndromic Thoracic Aortic Diseases: The REST Study.
evidence.snippet: Imaging revealed mild-to-moderate aortic dilation in 24% of relatives.

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in ACTA2, MYH11, MYLK, or PRKG1 impair vascular smooth muscle contraction and mechanotransduction, weakening the thoracic aorta.
nodes.id: Vascular smooth muscle contractile apparatus disruption

evidence.reference_title: HTAD patient pathway: Strategy for diagnostic work-up of patients and families with (suspected) heritable thoracic aortic diseases (HTAD). A statement from the HTAD working group of VASCERN.
evidence.snippet: Associated genes identified to date are classified into those encoding components of the (a) extracellular matrix (b) TGFβ pathway and (c) smooth muscle contractile mechanism.
